title: April 2022 Newsletter
summary: Meeting News | Cooperative Reports | IFQ Omnibus and Enforcement Committee Report | Recreational Quota Entity Funding Mechanism | Scallop Specs | CGOA Rockfish Adjustment | Bristol Bay Red King Crab | Bering Sea Fishery Ecosystem Plan Team and Taskforce reports | Ecosystem Committee | Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change | Best Scientific Information Available (BSIA) | Staff Tasking | Council Process Ideas for Change | Upcoming Meetings Meeting News Council Holds First Hybrid Meeting For the first time in two years, the Council met in-person, along with an online option for presenters and attendees. The meeting was held at the Anchorage Hilton, using double the meeting space allowing for extra distancing for both the Council members as well as the public following COVID protocols . Council administrative staff engineered the hybrid approach which also allowed remote public comment if a stakeholder indicated they would be testifying remotely. title: Fourteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta April 13
summary: On April 13, YKHC reports fourteen new cases of COVID-19 in the YK Delta. title: Alaska students compete at fourth annual Clean Energy Olympics
summary: Alaska students compete at fourth annual Clean Energy Olympics On March 26-31, 2022, Alaska students participated in the fourth annual Clean Energy Olympics competition. The Clean Energy Olympics is an engineering design challenge for Alaskan 4th-12th grade traditional and homeschool students. This year’s event was hybrid, with 10 teams with 36 students participating in person at Begich Middle School and 18 teams with 35 students p...

title: Voices of Alaska: Free Diver Chris Pollack
summary: Chris Pollack and his wife Amelia are free divers who live in Seldovia and are on a mission to share their passion for the sport. The post Voices of Alaska: Free Diver Chris Pollack appeared first on Alaska Magazine .
